name: Synthetic Response Generation Methods Search Pass

search_queries.result_count: screened first result page for each targeted query
screened first result page for each targeted query
screened first result page for each targeted query

inclusion_criteria: The source describes a method for generating synthetic, simulated, or AI-authored student or learner response text.
The source reports generation ingredients such as profiles, prompts, examples, target labels, corruption, agent state, or filtering.
exclusion_criteria: Exclude sources that only evaluate chatbot feedback without a recoverable synthetic student response generation method.
Exclude generic synthetic education datasets without response text or scoring/feedback relevance.
dedupe_method: Deduplicated by title, DOI, arXiv identifier, and canonical source page.

screening_counts.records_identified: at least 25 web results across targeted queries

screening_decisions.title: Leveraging Knowledge Profiles and Generative AI for Realistic Student Response Generation
screening_decisions.reason: Directly describes profile-based, rubric-aligned LLM generation strategies for synthetic student responses.

screening_decisions.title: Agent4Edu: Generating Learner Response Data by Generative Agents for Intelligent Education Systems
screening_decisions.reason: Describes LLM-powered generative agents with learner profiles, memory, and action modules for learner response generation.

screening_decisions.title: A Framework for Designing an AI Chatbot to Support Scientific Argumentation
screening_decisions.reason: Reports synthetic student inputs generated through Copilot prompts using high-quality, low-quality, and typo-conditioned examples.

screening_decisions.title: DREsS: Dataset for Rubric-based Essay Scoring on EFL Writing
screening_decisions.reason: Provides a non-LLM CASE corruption generation method for synthetic rubric-aligned essay training data.

screening_decisions.title: Calibrating Generative AI to Produce Realistic Essays for Data Augmentation
screening_decisions.reason: Compares prompt strategies for generating simulated essays from real student essays.

screening_decisions.title: Agent4Edu summary pages and repository mirrors
screening_decisions.reason: Duplicate metadata for the arXiv/AAAI source already included as candidate-agent4edu-2025.

screening_summary: Targeted bootstrap screening included five sources spanning profile- and rubric-conditioned LLM generation, generative-agent learner simulation, Copilot prompt generation from quality and typo-conditioned examples, CASE corruption-based essay generation, and prompt-strategy variation for simulated essays.
limitations: This was a targeted bootstrap pass, not a systematic review.
Screening prioritized web-accessible source pages and already available SSR baseline sources.
Some extraction depends on abstracts, metadata, or article summaries where full prompt templates and implementation details are not fully available in the indexed source page.
